review.evidence: The declarative map contains only approved bounded query/adoption/recovery routes; migrated reads execute through Application Kernel handlers over existing authorities; CLI-only force, destructive clean, install, init, and follow behavior remains outside the Agent catalog; normal JSON nests only data.agentResult, compact excludes it, and the full regression is green.

obligation_evidence.evidence: CLI query/adoption/recovery routes inject the existing config, registry, inspection, log, event, and journal authorities into one Application Kernel; no second state or policy backend was introduced.
Legacy schemaVersion 1 fields, mirrors, text, compact projection, and exit meanings remain stable while AgentOperationResult is additive only at data.agentResult.

Project-scoped reads receive Kernel-resolved trusted config/registry/journal context; unscoped global events preserve legacy CLI behavior but the Agent projection refuses before effects instead of inventing project authority.
Every migrated Agent result records cli/standalone/package-cli surface, runtime version/path, canonical state home, build identity, Agent protocol, and CLI schema provenance.
Adapter tests and real CLI probes keep outcome.kind and resource.status independent inside the nested normalized result, including safe unscoped-event refusal.
CLI schemaVersion remains 1 and is not derived from or collapsed into Agent protocol, package, config, state, or journal versions.
Real CLI fixtures prove additive normal JSON, legacy-only compact JSON, unchanged CLI-only reachability, and no semantic drift across existing CLI regressions.
